primary_sources.use: browser render-position evidence and Web Audio processing boundary
permission-gated microphone capture, constraints, and effective track settings

finding: No single browser-visible signal proves audible output. Strong acoustic evidence requires correlated render advance, diagnostic-tone detection, verified browser-controllable capture processing state, and a discriminating muted-output control; missing elements must downgrade rather than disappear.

verification.route: isolated Node.js test execution using byte-identical pre-commit source

uncertainty: Not yet integrated into the deployed Vercel runtime and not tested with an actual iPhone microphone session.
privacy_boundary: The classifier consumes derived classifications and booleans only; it requires no raw microphone samples, device labels, or identifiers to be persisted.

claim_boundary.supports: classification of correlated browser render and microphone-observed diagnostic-tone evidence
claim_boundary.does_not_prove: listener perception, adequate loudness, transducer identity, absence of platform-level processing, or cross-device repeatability
claim_boundary.falsification: repeat the protocol with muted output, changed route, and an off-target frequency; strong evidence must fail when diagnostic output is absent
